data.title: Davide Campari-Milano Non-GAAP EPS of €0.32, revenue of €3.05B; introduces FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Davide Campari-Milano press release (DVDCF): FY Non-GAAP EPS of €0.32.
  • Revenue of €3.05B (-0.7% Y/Y).
  • Outlook: The pace of underlying organic topline growth is set to continue in 2026, on track reaching mid- to high-single-digit revenue expansion as the Group advances towards its mid-term financial objectives, assuming a challenging yet stable operating environment.
  • Profitability remained robust, supported by contained organic accretion in adjusted EBIT margin, with a second-half weighting due to the front loading of advertising and promotion investments and the base effect of tariff.
  • The gross margin trajectory is expected to remain moderate, with tailwinds offset by an estimated tariffs impact of approximately €30 million based on current levels1

data.title: GM-backed driverless tech firm Momenta said to confidentially file for IPO in Hong Kong
data.description:
• Beijing Momenta Technology Co. is said to have filed confidentially for an initial public offering in Hong Kong, adding to a flurry of share sales in the Asian financial hub.
  • The autonomous driving tech startup, which is backed by General Motors (GM) and Tencent Holdings (TCEHY), is working with China International Capital Corp. (CNICF) and Deutsche Bank (DB) on the listing, Bloomberg News reported. The company has been meeting investors to gauge initial interest in the offering, the report said.

data.title: Trade court orders Trump administration to start refunding IEEPA tariffs
data.description:
A federal trade court judge has ordered the Trump administration to start issuing refunds for the tariffs that were struck down by the Supreme Court last month.

"All importers of record whose entries were subject to IEEPA [International Emergency Economic Powers Act] duties are entitled to the benefit" of the Supreme Court's decision, Judge Richard Eaton at the Court of International Trade said.

The ruling was for a case brought by Atmus Filtration, but Eaton said he will be the only judge who will hear cases pertaining to the refund of IEEPA tariffs.

"So there is no danger that another judge, even one in this court, will reach any contrary conclusions," he noted, and directed the Customs and Border Protection (CBP) to issue refunds after recalculating the duties importers paid and excluding the IEEPA tariffs.

Thousands of lawsuits seeking tariff refunds have been brought by companies including Costco (COST) and FedEx (FDX), many of which were filed before the Supreme Court's ruling.

The Trump administration collected more than $130B in IEEPA tariffs. It could be on the hook for up to $175B in refunds, according to the Penn Wharton Budget Model.

During the hearing for Atmus Filtration's case, the Justice Department's lawyer Claudia Burke said the refund process would be time-consuming as the CBP would have to manually go through millions of import entries.

"We live in the age of computers," Judge Eaton said, as reported by The Wall Street Journal. "It must be possible for Customs Service to program its computers so it doesn't need a manual review."

The Trump administration is expected to appeal the trade court judge's order.

data.title: Red Violet signals expansion into enterprise and public sector verticals while advancing AI-enabled platform
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Red Violet, Inc. (RDVT) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Derek Dubner, Chairman & CEO, opened the call noting "a record fourth quarter and a strong finish to 2025," highlighting disciplined execution, sustained momentum, and broad-based demand. He emphasized the adoption of Red Violet's cloud-native intelligence platform and the integration of the identity graph within customer workflows, stating "the strategic investments we have made over the past 2 years are translating into measurable operating performance."
  • Dubner reported revenue for the quarter was up 20% to $23.4 million, adjusted gross profit of $19.5 million with an 83% margin, adjusted EBITDA of $5.9 million (25% margin), adjusted net income of $3.1 million, and free cash flow of $3.7 million. IDI billable customer base grew by 169 sequentially to 10,022, and FOREWARN added 17,809 users to reach 390,018.
  • The CEO described Red Violet's competitive differentiation, emphasizing proprietary infrastructure: "Our platform is not a front-end application layered on top of a model. It is a full technology stack...all integrated to create and continuously refine a longitudinal identity graph developed and validated over many years."
  • He addressed AI's impact on the industry, asserting "AI strengthens and extends the advantages we have built. It does not replace them," and discussed the company's usage-based revenue model, noting "approximately 90% of our revenue is volume-driven."
  • Daniel MacLachlan, CFO, said "The fourth quarter marked a record finish to an exceptional year for Red Violet, defined by strong revenue growth, expanding margins and meaningful cash generation." He added, "We continue to scale the business both vertically, deepening adoption across existing markets, customers and use cases and horizontally, by introducing new products and expanding into new industries."

Outlook

  • MacLachlan reiterated that Red Violet does not provide formal guidance, but said, "we would expect 2026 to continue to deliver healthy top line expansion."
  • Management reaffirmed a focus on accelerating both horizontal and vertical expansion, with anticipated continued investment in product development, AI capabilities, and go-to-market initiatives.

Financial Results

  • Revenue was reported at $23.4 million for the quarter, up 20% over the prior year, with adjusted gross profit of $19.5 million (83% margin), adjusted EBITDA of $5.9 million (25% margin), and adjusted net income of $3.1 million ($0.21 per diluted share). Free cash flow was $3.7 million.
  • Annual revenue reached $90.3 million, adjusted gross profit $75.4 million, and adjusted EBITDA $31 million (34% margin). Free cash flow for the year was $18.2 million.
  • Contractual revenue accounted for 77% of total revenue in the quarter. Gross revenue retention was 95%. The company purchased 57,812 shares at an average price of $44.01 and had $16.4 million remaining under its repurchase program.

Q&A

  • Josh Nichols, B. Riley Securities: Asked about the enterprise pipeline and 2026 outlook. MacLachlan replied, "we've given some color on some recent wins...127 customers in excess of $100,000 in revenue a year...the pipeline continues to develop well, and we're converting into real meaningful customer wins."
  • Nichols followed up on law enforcement vertical growth and key opportunities for 2026. Dubner responded, "the end markets that I think that today, at least, we are most excited about, continue to be public sector and background screening support...we announced we won the largest payroll processor in Q3 last year. That contract kicks in this year."
  • Eric Martinuzzi, Lake Street Capital: Focused on higher-tier customers and prospects for large "whale-sized" accounts. MacLachlan confirmed "we have those opportunities now in the pipeline...the minimum commitment is probably around low to mid-6 figures starting in 2026, which is great. But we think the opportunity to expand that relationship goes into the 7 figures and plus."
  • Martinuzzi asked about upcoming data rights renewals. MacLachlan stated, "no, at this time, in the near term, there really is no material license agreements that are coming up for renewal."
  • Martinuzzi queried about growth outlook, noting mid-teens vs. 20% growth. MacLachlan reiterated that while no formal guidance is given, "our goal for the business is to continue to accelerate and drive the business and what you've seen consistently the last couple of years."
  • Martinuzzi inquired about capital allocation. Dubner said, "the likely use of cash is, definitely going to be investing in this business...horizontal expansion...vertical expansion...to get even further ingrained in their workflows."
  • David Polansky, Immersion Investments: Asked about revenue impact from new large customers. MacLachlan noted "some revenue from the payroll processor in Q4...contractual minimum commitment...does not start into 2026."
  • Polansky asked if high-spending customer growth came from new wins or existing spend. MacLachlan said, "it's a combination of both...some customers increasing volume...or it's a new customer win that happens to be a large, 6-figure plus year customer."
  • Polansky questioned sales and marketing headcount trends. MacLachlan explained, "we had a little bit of [churn] at the end of the year...the expectation would be very similar to that in 2026, a focus on product development, AI as well as go-to-market initiatives."
  • Polansky asked about AI use in operations. Dubner responded, "we announced in 2025, our strategic initiative to automate more...we would expect there is a lot more to do there...we are growing very quickly, and we're investing in the business, and that investment is for growth."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts showed a positive tone regarding customer expansion, enterprise pipeline, and the company's ability to buck seasonality, but pressed for specifics on growth rates, large client prospects, and capital allocation.
  • Management maintained a confident, measured tone in prepared remarks, using phrases such as "we are pleased," "we believe," and "we expect," and was direct but cautious in responses to growth rate and guidance questions. Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone shifted slightly more toward emphasizing strategic differentiation and longer-term opportunity.
  • Analysts' sentiment in the previous quarter was similarly positive, but current quarter questions focused more on sustainability of growth and monetization of large client wins.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Management reiterated themes of strong revenue growth, record financials, and product innovation, but in Q4, placed greater emphasis on the role of AI-enabled development and automation.
  • Q4 highlighted further expansion in the enterprise and public sector verticals, with the onboarding of the largest payroll processor beginning in 2026.
  • The company again bucked historical Q4 seasonality, noting sequential revenue growth, whereas Q3 anticipated possible seasonal impacts.
  • Analysts in both quarters focused on customer growth and enterprise pipeline, but in Q4, questions drilled deeper into the sustainability of high-tier customer growth, upcoming data rights renewals, and operational efficiency from AI.
  • The management’s tone remained confident and focused on long-term growth, with continued discussion of strategic investment and operational leverage.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management flagged continued pressure in the real estate vertical (excluding FOREWARN) due to elevated home prices and interest rates.
  • No material data license renewals are anticipated in the near term, reducing immediate risk on that front.
  • The company is investing in automation and AI to mitigate operational risks and improve scalability.

Final Takeaway

Red Violet closed 2025 with record revenue and profitability, balancing aggressive investment in AI-driven automation and product development with disciplined execution. Management signaled ongoing expansion in enterprise and public sector verticals, driven by a growing high-value customer base and continued platform adoption. The company emphasized confidence in its architecture and competitive positioning, projecting healthy top-line growth for 2026 while prioritizing investments to deepen integration and extend reach across core markets.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: StubHub targets $9.9B–$10.1B GMS for 2026 as company shifts to AI-driven direct issuance and refines marketing efficiency
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: StubHub Holdings, Inc. (STUB) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Eric Baker, Founder, CEO, & Chairman, described 2025 as a pivotal year for StubHub, highlighting the company's marketplace growth, strengthened competitive position, balance sheet transformation, and entry into the public markets. Baker stated, "As we enter 2026, StubHub remains a leading global ticketing marketplace for live events with durable advantages, scale and liquidity, structurally strong financial fundamentals and a diversified global footprint."
  • Baker emphasized StubHub's "category-defining brand and approximately 50% share of the secondary ticketing market in North America," and outlined a strategic shift for 2026 from a business development-led approach to a "more product-led strategy, building an AI-enabled technology-driven ecosystem that enables inventory to be contributed and managed with minimal operational burden." He clarified, "We will not be optimizing for immediate revenue growth, but for maximizing our revenue opportunity over the long term."
  • Baker also addressed regulatory developments, stating, "We continue to operate within a generally favorable status quo that supports open functioning resale markets across jurisdictions... approximately 10% of our GMS in 2025 was attributable to these types of high-demand concert ticket sales by resellers."
  • CFO Constance James reported, "In 2025, we delivered $9.2 billion of GMS, up 6% year-over-year. Excluding the Eras Tour, our GMS grew 18% year-over-year, reflecting the underlying performance of the business." James also noted market share gains in North America, expansion internationally, and raised operating efficiency with reduced debt and strong cash flow conversion.

Outlook

  • James provided annual guidance for 2026, stating, "We expect to grow GMS to between $9.9 billion and $10.1 billion, representing 9% growth at the midpoint and expand adjusted EBITDA to between $400 million and $420 million as our marketplace flywheel strengthen and operating leverage increases at scale."
  • Management clarified that no material revenue contribution from direct issuance or advertising is assumed in 2026 guidance, with both positioned as longer-term growth engines. James emphasized, "Our GMS growth formula is straightforward: North American market growth, incremental market share gains plus international growth."
  • Guidance is now issued on an annual, not quarterly, basis due to the seasonality and timing shifts in live event schedules.

Financial Results

  • For Q4 2025, StubHub generated $2.3 billion of GMS and $449 million in revenue, with adjusted gross margin at 83%. Adjusted sales and marketing expenses were $234 million or 52% of revenue.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter was $63 million (14% margin), and for the full year 2025 was $232 million (13% of revenue). James explained, "This result reflects... the deliberate investments we made during the year, both in market share acceleration and in building longer-term initiatives."
  • Free cash flow for 2025 represented nearly 70% conversion of adjusted EBITDA. The company reduced total debt by approximately 35%, ending the year with $1.2 billion in cash and cash equivalents, or $494 million net of payments due to sellers.
  • GAAP results included a nonrecurring noncash stock-based compensation expense of $1.4 billion related to the IPO and a nonrecurring noncash income tax expense of approximately $480 million.

Q&A

  • Douglas Anmuth, JPMorgan: Questioned if the 9% GMS growth guidance reflects slower core retail market growth. Baker responded that the North American market is strong, with continuing share gains and international expansion, and James emphasized the "all-in pricing overhang for the first 5 months."
  • Anmuth asked about the direct issuance strategy shift. Baker explained the focus is now on "eliminating friction on the product side" and developing "AI-assisted tools that automate workflows and simplify inventory management."
  • Eric Sheridan, Goldman Sachs: Asked about lessons from 2025 marketing investments. Baker stated, "We really were focused on finishing off and pushing this concept of the market share... we would see we would get to this relative market share... and start seeing this virtuous cycle occur." James noted improved marketing efficiency and expanded margins.
  • Justin Post, BofA: Inquired about the concert season, World Cup impact, and guidance. James said World Cup is a Tier 1 event in forecasts and that annualized analysis normalizes lumpiness from event timing.
  • Post also asked about regulatory environment and the Ticketmaster trial. Baker described regulatory focus on "a very narrow set of the market" and stated, "We do not bake in or anticipate any changes to what the status quo."
  • Mark Mahaney, Evercore: Asked about advertising traction. Baker said, "We started generating revenue and testing" in Q4 and will continue iterating with only "tens of millions" included in 2026 guidance.
  • Logan Whalley, TD Cowen: Asked about AI and agentic commerce. Baker highlighted that AI enables improved operational efficiency and customer experience, with James adding, "The team has already done a phenomenal job in terms of ops of support, really thinking about how we can create a level of efficiency."
  • Brian Pitz, BMO: Asked if dynamic pricing by primary ticketers is impacting secondary margins or volumes. Baker replied, "We have not seen any impact from those types of policies on our business."
  • Andrew Boone, Citizens: Asked about marketing efficiency and ReachPro. Baker explained ReachPro's benefits for seller workflow and data, and James stated, "You should continue to see increased benefit flowing through... expanding margins at the bottom line."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on sustainability of growth, regulatory risks, and strategy pivots, with a neutral to slightly positive tone—questions were constructive and sought clarity on strategic direction rather than expressing skepticism.
  • Management's tone was confident during prepared remarks and Q&A, repeatedly emphasizing strong fundamentals, "competitive position," and "deliberate" strategic choices. Phrases such as "we are confident" and "we believe" were frequent, indicating sustained optimism.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remained steady, but with increased specificity around the strategic transition to AI-driven product development and operating efficiency.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance is now annual rather than quarterly, with an explicit 2026 GMS target ($9.9B–$10.1B) and an EBITDA goal ($400M–$420M), compared to the absence of explicit 2026 guidance in Q3.
  • Strategic focus shifted from business development-led direct issuance to a product-led, AI-enabled approach in Q4, whereas Q3 emphasized building partnerships and expanding ReachPro adoption.
  • Analysts in both quarters questioned marketing efficiency, but in Q4, management provided more detail on the expected normalization and margin benefits.
  • The Q4 call provided more transparency on regulatory risks, product strategy, and advertising ramp, while reiterating the structural resilience and market share gains.
  • Both calls discussed international growth, but Q4 highlighted accelerated international expansion and operational leverage.

Risks and Concerns

  • Regulatory uncertainty in the U.S. secondary market and increased public discussion were cited, but management emphasized diversification and a lack of concentration as mitigating factors.
  • The company noted the seasonal and event-driven lumpiness of results, particularly from large events like the Eras Tour and World Cup.
  • Management highlighted operational risks in executing the AI-driven product strategy and acknowledged that direct issuance and advertising will not provide material revenue in 2026.

Final Takeaway

StubHub enters 2026 with a resilient core marketplace, a strengthened market share position around 50% in North America, and a focus on scaling its product-led, AI-driven direct issuance strategy. The company projects $9.9 billion to $10.1 billion in GMS and significant EBITDA expansion, supported by improved marketing efficiency and operational leverage. While regulatory discussions and live event timing create some uncertainty, management signals confidence in the durability and scalability of its business model, positioning StubHub for long-term growth beyond immediate revenue optimization.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Wix.com launches $1.75B modified Dutch auction to buy back shares at $80–$92
data.description:
• Wix.com (WIX) Thursday said that it has commenced a “modified Dutch auction” tender offer to purchase up to $1.75 billion of its ordinary shares at prices between $80 and $92 per share.
  • The tender offer will expire on April 1, 2026, unless extended or terminated. Wix believes repurchase aligns with its goal of maximizing shareholder value.
  • The tender offer is not contingent upon any minimum number of shares being tendered or any financing condition.

data.title: Merck KGaA Non-GAAP EPS of €8.34, revenue of €21.1B; introduces FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Merck KGaA press release (MKGAF): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of €8.34.
  • Revenue of €21.1B (-0.3% Y/Y), organic growth of +3.1%.
  • EBITDA pre grew to €6.1 billion; organic growth of +5.6%; foreign exchange effects of –5.0%.
  • Executive Board and Supervisory Board propose stable dividend of €2.20 per share.
  • Outlook 2026: Net sales of €20.0 – €21.1 billion and EBITDA pre of €5.5 – €6.0 billion amid considerable foreign exchange headwinds.

data.title: European markets dip over escalating concerns of conflict with Iran
data.description:
London (UKX) -0.25% to 10,540.

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.44% to 24,104. 

France (CAC:IND) -0.51% to 8,126. 

In other parts of Europe, retail sales in Hungary rose 3.5% Y/Y in January.

Switzerland’s non-seasonally adjusted unemployment rate held steady at 3.2% in February.

Industrial production in France rose 0.5% M/M in January.

The annual inflation rate in Sweden was unchanged at 0.5% in February.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) moved 0.27% lower to 611, slipping from some of the previous session’s gains as investors keep a close watch on the geopolitical developments in the Middle East. Reports also indicated that Iranian operatives had reached out to the US to explore potential peace talks, though Tehran later denied the outreach.

European natural gas futures rose toward €52/MWh on Thursday, moving back near a three-year high after Russian President Putin threatened to shut off gas supplies to Europe.

Coming up in the session: Euro Area retail sales expected shortly.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 4 basis points to 4.12%. 

Germany's 10-year yield was up 5 basis points to 2.79%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 6 basis points to 4.51%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Cracker Barrel outlines $85M–$100M adjusted EBITDA target for 2026 as guest metrics improve and loyalty program expands
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Cracker Barrel Old Country Store, Inc. (CBRL) Q2 2026

Management View

  • President and CEO Julie Masino reported, “Q2 total sales were $874.8 million and adjusted EBITDA was $38.2 million. Our entire team is executing our plan to: one, improve our operations; two, connect with guests through our menu, marketing and value proposition; and three, deliver cost savings to improve profitability.”
  • Masino highlighted operational improvements following leadership changes in October, sharing that the Google star rating reached 4.28 in Q2, the highest since Q2 of fiscal 2020. She noted improvements in food taste, service, and value scores, each up 4% to 5% year-over-year.
  • New and returning menu items, such as the breakfast burger, carrot cake, and sugar-cured ham dinners, exceeded expectations, with Masino stating, “We continue to use Front Porch Feedback, our guest feedback mechanism, and there are more returning favorites in the pipeline.”
  • The loyalty program, Cracker Barrel Rewards, now counts over 11 million members, accounting for over 40% of tracked sales. Masino described it as “a tremendous benefit for guests and an increasingly important tool in improving traffic.”
  • Masino detailed ongoing cost-saving measures, including a continued corporate restructuring aimed at returning G&A closer to historical levels as a percentage of sales.
  • Senior VP & CFO Craig Pommells stated, “For Q2, we reported total revenue of $874.8 million, which was down 7.9% from the prior year quarter. Restaurant revenue decreased 7.5% to $694.3 million. Comparable store restaurant sales decreased by 7.1%, which included a traffic decline of 10.1%.”

Outlook

  • Pommells provided fiscal 2026 guidance: “Total revenue of $3.24 billion to $3.27 billion, pricing of approximately 4% and lower menu mix resulting from higher discounts, commodity inflation of 2% to 2.5%, and hourly inflation of 2.5% to 3%.”
  • Full year adjusted EBITDA is anticipated at $85 million to $100 million. Capital expenditures are now expected between $105 million and $115 million.
  • Pommells commented on traffic expectations: “In the full year, we would expect traffic to be somewhere in the neighborhood of negative 8.5% to negative 9.5%.”

Financial Results

  • Restaurant average check increased 3.4%, with pricing up 4.2% and menu mix negative due to higher discounts. Off-premise sales made up 23.6% of restaurant sales.
  • Retail revenue was $180.5 million, with comparable store retail sales down 9.2%.
  • Cost of goods sold rose: total COGS reached 33.5% of revenue, restaurant COGS 27.4% of sales, and retail COGS 56.8% of retail sales. Labor expenses increased to 36.1% of revenue, primarily due to sales deleverage and wage inflation of 2%.
  • Adjusted general and administrative expenses were 4.9% of revenue, with savings driven by lower incentive compensation and cost savings initiatives.
  • A noncash store impairment charge of $400,000 related to Maple Street stores was recorded.
  • GAAP earnings per diluted share were $0.06; adjusted EPS was $0.25. Adjusted EBITDA margin was 4.4% of revenue.
  • Debt at quarter end was $531.5 million.

Q&A

  • Dennis Geiger, UBS: Asked about the pace of traffic improvement. Pommells responded, “We do believe the underlying trend is gradually improving. As we shared, January did better than November and December... and we're encouraged by an even better start to February.”
  • Geiger, UBS: Questioned use of guest metrics as recovery indicators. Masino said, “We are really encouraged by the improvement in everything you mentioned... we're moving in the right direction.”
  • Jon Tower, Citi: Inquired about marketing tactics with lower ad spend. Masino replied, “Driving traffic is about much more than just advertising... we have a holistic plan to really reach them in those channels to bring them back and build their trust.”
  • Tower, Citi: Sought tariff outlook update. Pommells said, “We do expect it to be a smaller tariff impact, a little bit this year... but there is a lag with all of that, and the rate change is a bit more modest.”
  • Jake Bartlett, Truist: Asked about traffic guidance. Pommells answered, “Our thinking right now is in the full year, we would expect traffic to be somewhere in the neighborhood of negative 8.5% to negative 9.5%.”
  • Bartlett, Truist: Questioned brand sentiment recovery. Masino admitted, “We are a little bit below sort of the average for casual at the moment, and so really working to claw that back and improve our trends there.”
  • Sara Senatore, BofA: Asked about gas prices’ impact on traffic. Pommells noted, “We have a bit more exposure to travel, which exposes us to gas, but we also have more exposure to folks that are over 65, and they are likely to have a benefit as well from the tax changes.”
  • Todd Brooks, Benchmark: Queried source of traffic lift. Masino explained, “We also were able to capture a meaningful percentage of lapsed guests that we hadn't seen in Q1 that came back in Q2. So that... feels good to us because... increasing frequency with people that know us and are already in our ecosystem is really important to us.”
  • Brooks, Benchmark: Inquired about holiday meal performance. Masino reported, “We did almost $110 million in sales on Thanksgiving week, which was a big week for us. Thanksgiving traffic was in line with the rest of the month.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a cautiously optimistic tone, frequently pressing on the pace of traffic recovery, brand sentiment, and the effectiveness of marketing and promotions.
  • Management showed a slightly more confident and upbeat tone compared to the previous quarter, emphasizing improvements in guest experience metrics and operational execution, but acknowledged ongoing challenges. Masino stated, “We are really encouraged by the improvement in everything you mentioned... we're moving in the right direction.”
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management displayed increased focus on leading indicators and a more concrete discussion of guest metrics and cost management, with a more measured optimism now supported by early positive trends.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language narrowed, with EBITDA forecast now at $85 million to $100 million versus the prior $70 million to $110 million range, and revenue guidance lifted slightly to $3.24 billion–$3.27 billion from $3.2 billion–$3.3 billion.
  • Strategic focus shifted further toward leveraging the loyalty program and targeted menu improvements, while cost controls and G&A reductions progressed.
  • Analysts placed more emphasis on the sources of traffic recovery and the sustainability of guest metric gains, while management provided more detailed commentary on leading indicators and segmented guest engagement.
  • Management sentiment became more confident due to observed improvements in guest metrics and operational execution, a notable change from the more defensive tone last quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Ongoing traffic declines and the pace of recovery remain key risks, with Pommells highlighting that “the rate and level of our traffic recovery as well as the level of investment required remain key drivers.”
  • Tariff volatility and its impact on retail margins were discussed, though management expects a smaller effect this year.
  • Cost inflation in commodities and labor persists but is expected to moderate versus previous guidance.
  • The effectiveness of reduced marketing spend in conjunction with digital and loyalty-based engagement strategies remains uncertain.

Final Takeaway

Cracker Barrel management remains focused on operational improvements, guest experience, and disciplined cost management, with early signs of progress in guest metrics and loyalty engagement. While traffic and revenue trends are gradually improving, management acknowledges more work ahead to regain momentum, supported by a narrowed EBITDA outlook and targeted investments in menu innovation and digital engagement.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Deutsche Post GAAP EPS of Є3.04, revenue of Є82.86B; introduces FY26 outlook, raises dividend
data.description:
• Deutsche Post AG press release (DHLGY): FY GAAP EPS of Є3.04.
  • Revenue of Є82.86B (-1.6% Y/Y).
  • Group exceeds guidance with strong free cash flow (excluding M&A) of EUR 3.2 billion (FY 2024: EUR 3.0 billion).
  • Supervisory Board and Board of Management propose dividend increase to EUR 1.90 per share (FY 2024: EUR 1.85 per share).
  • DHL Group expects an operating profit above EUR 6.2 billion and free cash flow (excluding M&A) of around EUR 3 billion for 2026.
